task_completion.description: The agent selects the target dive dated September 2011 from the logbook list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive list in the application shows a dive from September 2011 highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: The agent updates the location of the selected dive to 'Blue Hole'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The location input field in the dive details/edit panel contains the text 'Blue Hole'.

task_completion.description: The agent updates the dive mode to 'Freedive'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive mode or dive type dropdown/input in the dive details panel is set to 'Freedive' (or 'Apnea').

task_completion.description: The agent applies the edits to the specific dive.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a save button, checkmark, or 'Apply' button within the dive edit panel to confirm the changes to the dive's metadata.

task_completion.description: The agent saves the entire logbook.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks 'File' -> 'Save', clicks a Save icon on the main toolbar, or uses a save keyboard shortcut (e.g., Ctrl+S) in the application.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual dive log GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the graphical user interface of the dive log application (e.g., Subsurface) being used, rather than a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing the underlying data file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editor (like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code) is shown editing the raw .ssrf or XML logbook file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to modify the logbook.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to execute scripts (e.g., Python, sed, awk) that directly alter the logbook data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected location to be entered is 'Blue Hole'. The expected dive mode is 'freedive' (accept 'Freedive' or 'Apnea'). Note: The task description mentions 'Dive #8', but in the actual sample data, the last dive in September 2011 is Dive #90 (and the 8th dive in the file is #88). You should award points if the agent selects the last dive in September 2011 or any dive it reasonably identifies as the target based on the prompt's conflicting instructions.
